rulings.Case Title: JOHN BLACK et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 17, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE DRYWALL, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The Motion for Summary Judgment is denied because despite shifting of the burden to Plaintiff, the McCollum and Ay declarations contain admissible testimony creating a triable issue of fact. McCollum saw Defendant employee perform drywall work within seven feet of decedent (paragraphs 6, 7), Ay's declaration (paragraphs 10, 11) contains expert opinion. Defendant's evidentiary objections Nos. 1,2,3,4,5,6 & 7 are overruled. Plaintiff's evidentiary objection to the Medeiros declaration is overruled; parts of it may be inadmissible, but Plaintiff utterly fails to object to these specific statements. The so-called D'Amico rule does not control here; McCollum deposition was taken in a different case seven years ago and lacks specific examination about decedent. The so-called Disola rule does not control here. Statements of Defendant's employees to McCollum constitute admissions. Moreover, he simply describes his observation of the name on vehicles. The Court does grant summary adjudication as to Plaintiff's second, third and Sixth causes of action. =(302KOPP/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN SOCHA VS. HARRY HOM et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 17, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT HARRY HOM, BEATRICE HOM, and, HOGAN AND VEST, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Moot due to demurrer filing. =(302KOPP/JW)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN SOCHA VS. HARRY HOM et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 17, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT HARRY HOM, BEATRICE HOM, and, HOGAN AND VEST, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer to the first cause of action is overruled. Defendant fails to show the governing period is one year and Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts to support a rent ordinance violation under the applicable liberal pleading standard. The demurrer to sixth cause of action is sustained. Plaintiff must allege more than creation and afflection of debris with Defendant's knowlege and permission. Intentional inflection of emotional distress requires pleading of facts constituting extreme and outrageous behavior transcending bounds of decency. As desired, Plaintiff may file and serve a First Amended Complaint by April 27, 2009. If she does not, Defendants shall file and serve their answers by April 30, 2009. =(302KOPP/JW)
